falsification_ledger.claim_under_stress: If mechanism A is right, conditions should improve quickly once alternate ports regains workable throughput or the most binding part of 'Physical throughput or congestion limits' is relaxed
falsification_ledger.hidden_assumption: The main constraint really is concentrated at alternate ports rather than being displaced into substitute actors, nodes, or decision processes
falsification_ledger.how_it_could_fail: Physical throughput or congestion limits or a closely related bottleneck remains binding outside alternate ports, so nominal recovery at the focal node does not translate into real system recovery
falsification_ledger.what_evidence_would_break_it: Evidence that downstream delays, shortages, or performance gaps remain elevated after measurable recovery at alternate ports would falsify the claim that direct throughput restoration is sufficient

falsification_ledger.claim_under_stress: If mechanism B is right, downstream disruption should persist after alternate ports stabilizes because actors continue hoarding, reprioritizing, or mis-coordinating around the expected risk
falsification_ledger.hidden_assumption: Actor behavior rather than direct capacity loss is doing most of the explanatory work, and customs is still reacting to expected risk rather than current conditions
falsification_ledger.how_it_could_fail: Once the first-order bottleneck clears, the second- and third-order effects unwind quickly, showing that coordination frictions were secondary rather than dominant
falsification_ledger.what_evidence_would_break_it: If substitute nodes decongest and exposed actors normalize behavior soon after the first-order shock eases, the expectation-and-coordination account loses force

surprise_ledger.surprise: A secondary node or actor outside alternate ports becomes the real bottleneck once attention and mitigation resources concentrate on the headline disruption
surprise_ledger.why_shallow_analysis_misses_it: Shallow analysis stays fixed on the initial shock and underweights the way rerouting, substitution, and political attention can create a new constraint elsewhere

surprise_ledger.surprise: An institutional or behavioral response from customs amplifies the third-order effects more than the physical disruption itself
surprise_ledger.why_shallow_analysis_misses_it: Surface-level tracing often assumes actors passively absorb shocks, even though defensive prioritization, compliance choices, or public signaling can magnify downstream consequences
